submission.questionnaireText:
Gray Randolph
Adult 01/01/2000 (25)  Schedule appointment  Edit

1. Initial Health Survey (complete at your earliest convenience)
09/17/2025 at 1:56 PM
Note



1. Do you gain weight easily?
(1) I do not experience this
2. [IF SO] Where do you typically gain weight on your body?
Not Applicable
3. Do you feel "puffy," i.e your fat tends to be soft and feel like water retention?
(1) I do not experience this
4. Do you struggle with unintended weight loss?
(1) I do not experience this
5. [WOMEN ONLY] Do you accumulate cellulite?
Not Applicable
6. Do you struggle with low appetite?
(2) This is a somewhat bothersome issue for me
7. Do you tend to wake up when sleeping?
(5) This is an extremely bothersome issue for me
8. If you tend to wake up when sleeping, is it: within the first few hours of falling asleep OR later on in the night OR both?
Later in the night
9. If you tend to wake up when sleeping, is it to: urinate OR eat something OR drink something OR seemingly no reason?
Urinate
10. If you tend to wake up when sleeping, do you find that you are hot or sweaty upon waking?
No
11. Do you NOT dream?
(1) I do not experience this
12. Do you experience nightmares or sleep paralysis?
(2) This is a somewhat bothersome issue for me
13. Do you have trouble falling asleep?
(1) I do not experience this
14. Do you feel like you DON'T get a restful sleep?
(3) This is a fairly bothersome issue for me
15. Do you frequently feel cold, especially at the extremities (fingertips, toes, and nose)?
(1) I do not experience this
16. [WOMEN ONLY] Do you frequently have painful menstrual cramps?
Not Applicable
17. [WOMEN ONLY] Do you frequently have irregular menstruation or suffer from premenstrual syndrome?
Not Applicable
18. Do you suffer from chronic congestion without mucus?
(5) This is an extremely bothersome issue for me
19. Do you frequently experience mucus buildup in the throat or nose?
(5) This is an extremely bothersome issue for me
20. [IF SO] Is it exacerbated after eating?
Yes
21. Do you suffer from dental problems such as bad breath, cavities, and gum inflammation?
(1) I do not experience this
22. Do you often find that you are irritable or easily agitated?
(3) This is a fairly bothersome issue for me
23. Do you suffer from "brain fog," low energy or feel like you do not have mental clarity?
(4) This is a very bothersome issue for me
24. Do you tend to have an "afternoon crash" after eating lunch?
(3) This is a fairly bothersome issue for me
25. Do you often feel "hyper aware," jittery, or restless?
(4) This is a very bothersome issue for me
26. Do you notice the sensation of your heart pounding or difficulty breathing?
(2) This is a somewhat bothersome issue for me
27. Have you had a loss of pleasure in activities you normally enjoy?
(5) This is an extremely bothersome issue for me
28. Have you noticed a lack of sexual interest or desire?
(4) This is a very bothersome issue for me
29. [MEN ONLY] Have you lost morning erections?
(2) This is a somewhat bothersome issue for me
30. Do you have skin issues such as dry skin, eczema, or acne?
(1) I do not experience this
31. Can you trace a worsening of your symptoms back to a traumatic event?
Yes
32. Do you have specific food cravings? If so, please list the foods you crave.
Yes
chocolate, bread, most sugar I could binge on, even though I don't most of the time.
33. Do you have a history of mental health issues? If so, please list them.
Yes
Not until this started in 2023. When I started experiencing symptoms, I also began to experience panic attacks and depression.
34. Do you experience constipation?
(5) This is an extremely bothersome issue for me
35. Do you experience nausea?
(3) This is a fairly bothersome issue for me
36. Do you experience bloating (subjective feeling)?
(5) This is an extremely bothersome issue for me
37. Do you experience distention (visible expansion of the abdomen)?
(5) This is an extremely bothersome issue for me
38. Do you experience diarrhea?
(5) This is an extremely bothersome issue for me
39. Do you experience belching?
(1) I do not experience this
40. Do you experience flatulence?
(5) This is an extremely bothersome issue for me
41. Do you experience pain in your upper abdomen?
(3) This is a fairly bothersome issue for me
42. Do you experience pain in your right upper quadrant?
(1) I do not experience this
43. Do you experience pain in your lower abdomen?
(5) This is an extremely bothersome issue for me
44. Are you aware of any specific foods that trigger your symptoms? If so, please list them.
Yes
Yes - eating lots of frozen fruit, eating lots of fruit in general, eating sandwiches, caffeine, alcohol, almost all bread, coconut, coffee, processed ice cream, raisins, almost anything with high fiber. PHGG supplement nearly destroyed me, caused a massive histamine response.
LMNT electrolytes.
Most cheese.
Pasta.
Peanut Butter.
Granola.
45. Do you experience dry stools?
(1) I do not experience this
46. Do you experience acid reflux?
(2) This is a somewhat bothersome issue for me
47. Do your symptoms worsen IMMEDIATELY after eating?
(4) This is a very bothersome issue for me
48. Do your symptoms worsen 1-3 hours after eating?
(5) This is an extremely bothersome issue for me
49. Do symptoms alleviate after a bowel movement?
Sometimes
50. Have you tried anything in the past to alleviate your symptoms? These can be dietary changes, supplements, prescriptions or anything else. If so, please list them, and how helpful (or unhelpful) they were for you. Please be as specific as possible, including specific foods and brands and dosages of supplements, as well as what symptoms they affected.
Yes
Gray's Health Journey: Comprehensive Summary
Timeline of Events
October 2022 - Initial Onset
Original panic attack triggered the cascade of symptoms
Severe illness for one week with:
Tons of mucus and sore throat
Extreme brain fog (felt like lost 15-20 IQ points)
Complete inability to multitask anymore
Excessive mucus production
Subjective feeling that "brain changed"
Doctors indicated no active illness despite persistent symptoms
Post-October 2022 - Early Symptoms
Gastrointestinal: Extreme IBS developed
Urological: Very frequent urination (up to 4 times per night)
Psychological: String of panic attacks throughout the year
May 2023 - Psychedelic Intervention Attempt
LSD trial: Taken to attempt to resolve what was thought to be a psychological issue
Results:
Horrible experience
Felt like it "fried" the nervous system
Possibly added much more tension and pressure to nervous system
Late May 2023 - Additional Nervous System Stress
Cannabis edible on plane: Felt like it hurt the nervous system
Sensation that nervous system was under immense stress, "fried"
Summer 2023 - Symptom Escalation
Panic attacks worsened significantly
New symptoms appeared:
Massive eye bags upon waking
Facial inflammation
Daytime fatigue and low energy
ENT consultation: Confirmed nasal and throat inflammation of unknown origin
October 2023 - Surgical Intervention
Nasal surgery performed to improve breathing
Results: Only ~20% improvement
Continued issues with panic attacks and breathing
New symptom: Pain in stomach when breathing at night
Deep breathing causes lower stomach discomfort
April/May 2024 - Mexico Trip - Crisis Point
Dramatic shift from frequent IBS to complete constipation (0 bowel movements in 3 days)
Severe bloating ("looked pregnant")
April/May 2024 - Japan Medical Consultation
Prescribed Levofloxacin (antibiotic)
Results:
No improvement in stomach symptoms
Complete breathing clearance for 2 weeks
Stomach felt "destroyed" afterward
Extreme IBS returned
July 2024 - Antimicrobial Protocol Attempt
Chris Voutsas protocol:
1.5-2 months of Ultimate GI repair from LVLup health
Components included:
BPC-157
Antimicrobials (berberine, oregano oil, coconut oil)
Preparatory phase with NAC and enzymes (nattokinase, bromelain)
Results:
Protocol discontinued due to severe digestive deterioration
Digestion became so impaired could only digest eggs
Eventually even eggs weren't being digested properly
December 2024 - SIBO Treatment
Prescribed Rifaximin (SIBO-specific antibiotic)
Completed full course with no effect whatsoever
March 2025 - Second Antibiotic Trial
Prescribed Azithromycin (500mg for 3 days)
Results:
Dramatic improvement in breathing and IBS for 2 weeks
All symptoms returned after 2 weeks (sneezing, acne, etc.)
June 2025 - Antifungal Treatment
Protocol: Digestive enzymes + NAC followed by 1 month of Nystatin
Results:
No improvement or resolution of symptoms
Current Persistent Symptoms
1. Gastrointestinal
Constant IBS
Food sensitivities
Respiratory
Constant sneezing
Poor nasal breathing
Stomach pain with deep breathing at night
Neurological/Psychological
Racing mind
Anxiety
Brain fog
Reduced mental clarity
Other
Low energy
Skin issues/acne
Facial inflammation
Key Observations & Patterns
Antibiotic Response Pattern
Two different antibiotics (Levofloxacin and Azithromycin) provided temporary but complete relief of breathing symptoms
Both provided approximately 2 weeks of improvement
Rifaximin (SIBO-specific) had no effect
Suggests possible chronic infection or dysbiosis affecting respiratory system
Histamine Connection
Symptoms consistent with histamine intolerance
Trigger foods are high in histamine or histamine-releasing
Question remains: What's causing the histamine intolerance?
Fasting Response
3-day fast resulted in feeling "amazing" the day after
Suggests digestive rest or reduction in inflammatory foods provides relief
Interconnected Symptoms
Clear connection between:
Food intolerance
Nasal swelling
Anxiety/brain fog
Skin issues
Summary
This appears to be a complex, multi-system condition that began after an acute illness in October 2022. The condition potentially worsened following psychedelic interventions in May 2023 that appeared to further stress the nervous system. The temporary response to certain antibiotics suggests an infectious or dysbiotic component, while the food sensitivities and systemic symptoms point toward histamine intolerance or mast cell involvement. The interconnected nature of respiratory, digestive, and neurological symptoms creates a complex symptom pattern that has persisted for over two years despite various treatment attempts.
51. Are you currently taking any supplements? If so, please list them and be as specific as possible, including the brand and dosage of any supplement. Please mention how helpful (or unhelpful) you perceive them to be.
No
52. Are you currently taking any medications? If so, please list them and be as specific as possible, including the brand and dosage of any medication.
No
53. Are you currently diagnosed with any medical conditions? If so, please list them.
No
54. Please describe your diet. What foods do you typically eat, and in what amounts? Are these foods prepared by you, or are they pre-packaged, or from a restaurant? At what times do you typically eat?
I typically eat eggs (3-4) in the morning with Raw Milk, usually for lunch I will have a 300-400g chicken tortilla with cheese, guacamole + onions and tabasco that I make at home, or I'll have sushi from the grocery store. Typically for dinner I'll have some type of steak, beef, or meats with potatoes, sauerkraut or similar. I also tended to eat out a lot, because I spent the past 6 months abroad in Europe, so the meals could vary quite a lot. However I mostly stick to meats like Turkey, Chicken, Beef, Pork, and also Perogi, or typical Polish Cuisine.

55. Please describe your lifestyle. Do you get outside often? Do you exercise? How stressful is your day-to-day life due to work or other factors?
I am a pretty active person, usually I spent 1-2hrs outside per day and I've averaged 11,000 steps per day over the last 6 months. I usually go to the gym 5x per week, 45mins to 1 hr of mostly bodybuilding / aesthetic type movements.

I would say that I am quite stressed due to work and lifestyle factors, but practicing mindfulness like meditation daily to detach from it.

56. What are your health primary goals you are looking achieve?
I am looking to have proper digestion so I can feel amazing, fired up, have more energy. Also to fix my breathing with I believe is a histamine response from bad gut health, and to fix my sleep. it is very restless.

57. Have you ever had food poisoning before? If so, when?
Yes - I believe in Mexico of May 2024 and Thailand August 2024. Both times were pretty horrible, but in Thailand I was hospitalized and severely panicked.

58. Please list your height, weight and waist measurements.
6 foot 1 - 170lbs, and I am a 31 waist.

59. Please list any other information you think would help us help you.
I have a lot of bloodwork documents and gut tests I would like to attach.

Clinician: Maria Dees
Edit  
9:28 PM
The client won’t upload their Cronometer data because he says he always eats the same things:
”I typically eat eggs (3-4) in the morning with Raw Milk, usually for lunch I will have a 300-400g chicken tortilla with cheese, guacamole + onions and tabasco that I make at home, or I'll have sushi from the grocery store. Typically for dinner I'll have some type of steak, beef, or meats with potatoes, sauerkraut or similar. I also tended to eat out a lot, because I spent the past 6 months abroad in Europe, so the meals could vary quite a lot. However I mostly stick to meats like Turkey, Chicken, Beef, Pork, and also Perogi, or typical Polish Cuisine”.

---

Appointment #1
With: Shannon Sirois 
Billing code: 
consulting
Edit
2:00 PM – 3:00 PM
Progress Note 
Gray has had a long-standing battle with gut issues tracing back to when he was a kid. He does remember being on a lot of different antibiotics and having a sensitive stomach, but never like it is now. As you can see (he has a great time-line in his note) this really took off in 2022 when he got a sickness that completely knocked him on his butt. From this point on, he had debilitating gut and energy issues that plagued him. Through this process he’s had many tests done as you can see some are uploaded. His gut has pathogens, most recent ones were cryptosporidium, e. coli, staph and he is missing some favorable microbes. He also stated that for how athletic and competitive he is, he knows that his testosterone is on the lower side. His DHEA-s was also low normal. Basically this illness completely upended his system and he’s never been the same.

Since then, he’s had courses of antibiotics, anti-fungals etc. The anti-fungals did nothing but the antibiotics cured him for a week or two at a time. He said that each time he took an antibiotic he felt like he was finally “cured” but then as soon as they wore off he would be right back to square one. Most recently he was in Mexico and found the anti-parasitic that is specific for Cryptosporidium. He said that again he got major relief and for the first time in months he’s not having the back and forth diarrhea/constipation issues, and now he can tolerate more foods like raw milk. I’m hoping that he wiped out that infection and we can really work on rebuilding his gut from here. This kid has literally done everything. The other thing I want to note is that herbal protocols always make him worse, and given his high calprotectin and the underlying inflammation in his gut this does not surprise me.

He did not take his temps, so he’s going to do that in the next few days and hopefully get back to me. His thyroid labs were not great, and given all of his other sx’s and high cholesterol, there’s really no way he’s thriving via thyroid hormone right now. We ordered thyroid labs for him and he’s going to do that ASAP. I think optimizing this will be very helpful.

As you can see from his following outline that I will attach, he’s had clear histamine issues, serotonin issues (given the reaction he had to edibles and psychadelics) and has been on protocols that have probably just worsened these sx’s. When I explained to him about the serotonin, he had never heard about high serotonin in the gut causing these issues. He was under the impression that serotonin is a good thing! I gave him a little overview of our take on this, but feel free to provide any extra attachments to the report if we have links.

The one things he is really struggling with is at night. He is still waking 8-10 times per night with intense pain/cramping and bloating. He says that sometimes he has to wear bigger pants because his stomach is sticking out so much. He has lean tissue with abs, but his abdomen is huge and protrudes outward. At night he can feel this and it feels like it’s maxxing out and the pain is intense, which is why he wakes up repeatedly. I’m hoping this was a result of the parasite, and the the meds he just took may resolve this.

This is a clear-cut case. Optimize the gut, support the thyroid, support testosterone production and this kid will be flying. I’m really hoping that he cleared things up with this most recent anti-parasitic. Fingers crossed, but he’s been good since he took it.

Food:
I typically eat eggs (3-4) in the morning with Raw Milk, usually for lunch I will have a 300-400g chicken tortilla with cheese, guacamole + onions and tabasco that I make at home, or I'll have sushi from the grocery store. Typically for dinner I'll have some type of steak, beef, or meats with potatoes, sauerkraut or similar. I also tended to eat out a lot, because I spent the past 6 months abroad in Europe, so the meals could vary quite a lot. However I mostly stick to meats like Turkey, Chicken, Beef, Pork, and also Perogi, or typical Polish Cuisine

Via messaging:

Me:

This is a bit cheesy, but it really works. It's only 5 min so I like to have people do this anytime they have gut distress, feel anxious or even right before bed. Since it's only 5 min it's easy to fit this in daily….and a few times a day if necessary. It's called box breathing and it utilizes the left nostril like we talked about for the "parasympathetic" recruitment.

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=uQgE2KkKqAE

Me: Also, what was the name of the antiparisitic that you took while in Mexico?

Him: It was Nitazoxanide

Plan:

One of the things he said he participated in was a 40 min breathwork session and after that he literally thought he was cured. ALL of his sx’s went away for a day. This unfortunately didn’t last, but I did explain to him how this can calm down an overstimulated sympathetic system and improve vagal tone. He’s going to start going back to sessions and I attached a quick version of it for him via youtube as seen above.

Thyroid support: prior to testing (I think he’ll need NDT) how about some minerals for the selenium and zinc? Let’s do Anderson minerals: half dose with two biggest meals (to equal full dose total) and 15-20 mg of zinc carnosine once per day (nutricost has 15 mg I believe)

Forefront health liquid B vitamins: sublingual and Forefront health liquid A,D,K topical.

He’s currently taking Ultimate GI repair. I feel like he needs something to re-establish/colonize things. HMO’s?

I also suspect H pylori given his reaction to PHGG and also his immediate reaction to eating foods, this usually signifies fermenting directly in the stomach so let’s do mastic gum and L. rhamnosus GG from SuperSmart.

DAO enzyme: he has strong histamine reactions, so take this prior to meals 2 x per day. Designs for Health is a good one!

I’m really hoping that the anti parasitic he took a week ago did the trick, but if not let’s include info on how to obtain minocycline just in case.

Aloe vera and activated charcoal. I’d like him to start using aloe vera juice once per day and also obtain some activated charcoal to use as needed when his gut flares up. I’m going to message him to ask if he’s ever used the charcoal, but I may even have him do this for 3 nights in a row NOW just to mop up any die off from the parasite. Let’s still include a brand in the report, please.

Testing: I ordered zinc, copper and total thyroid panel that should be done soon.

His message to me this morning 10/16

I have great news, I'm not kidding you yesterday was the happiest day of my life I had in two years. Maybe its a sign of healing, or what I did and how the day was structured, but my mood was so good. I took 20,000 of IU of Vitamin D + k2 in the morning and did power yoga. I was so happy and full of energy the whole day! Usuallyy I don't take the Vit D like that but I did because my friend had some here, and maybe it was either that, the yoga, or I am simply healing!

---

Clinician: Shannon Sirois
Edit  
11:15 AM
let’s also add liquid zinc from Pure Encapsulations: 15 mg zinc picolinate. He’s going to do half of the capsule morning, noon and evening (7.5 mg each meal basically). I don’t want to overdo the zinc, I have been messaging with him because his hair copper was off the charts, which means he likely has a lot of free copper and I don’t want to drive that out in a hurry with high doses of zinc.
